---
title: "The Coffee Shop"
description: "An imaginary coffee shop with ~39,500 chairs, every chair a sociologist or political scientist, every conversation a citation. 1950 → 2025."
date: 2026-05-10
author: "Derek Wakefield"
categories: ["network analysis", "sociology", "political science", "genealogy"]
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    toc: false
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <style>
            #title-block-header { display: none; }
            main.content, .content { padding: 0 !important; margin: 0 !important; max-width: 100% !important; }
            body > .page-columns { display: block; }
          </style>
---

```{=html}
<div class="cs-page" id="csPage">
  <aside class="cs-sidebar" id="csSidebar" aria-label="Coffee Shop information">
    <div class="cs-sidebar-inner">

      <header class="cs-header">
        <h1>The Coffee Shop</h1>
        <p class="cs-meta">network analysis · sociology · political science · genealogy</p>
        <p class="cs-stats">
          <span class="cs-stat-num">~39,500</span> chairs ·
          <span class="cs-stat-num">5,500</span> heads of school ·
          <span class="cs-stat-num">1950 → 2025</span>
        </p>
      </header>

      <blockquote class="cs-epigraph">
        <em>A small voice in the dark moves more than it knows. Chirp anyway.</em>
      </blockquote>

      <section class="cs-section">
        <p>Imagine a coffee shop that opened in <strong>1950</strong> and never closed.</p>
        <p>Every chair is a sociologist or political scientist. Some have been there since the doors opened — Coleman in one corner, Parsons at the bar in the early years, Merton in the back booth, Massey by the window watching people walk past. Others arrived later — Mendelberg ordered her first espresso in the mid-90s and has been here ever since. The newest baristas just clocked in last year.</p>
        <p>The shop has <strong>~39,500 chairs</strong> and they're all full.</p>
        <p>Most conversations are <em>citations</em> — someone overhearing what someone else said and writing it down. But some conversations are something rarer: the kind where one scholar trains another, where a sentence in 1953 ends up shaping a sentence in 2018 because the second person learned how to think from the first.</p>
        <p>This page is a map of those conversations.</p>
      </section>

      <section class="cs-section">
        <h2>What you're looking at</h2>
        <ul>
          <li><strong>~39,500 dots.</strong> Each dot is an author who published in a flagship sociology or political-science journal between 1950 and 2025, or meets the citation-impact bar in the expansion crawl.</li>
          <li><strong>Vertical position = the year of their first publication.</strong> 1950 at the top, 2025 at the bottom.</li>
          <li><strong>Color = field & tier.</strong> Reds for sociology, blues for political science. Darker = top-flight journal.</li>
          <li><strong>Lines = mentor → mentee edges.</strong>
            <ul>
              <li><strong>Bold purple</strong> — Wikipedia-verified advisor/student pairs from top-500 author bios. Gold-standard.</li>
              <li><strong>Thin gray</strong> — inferred via Sinatra-style co-authorship asymmetry (≥5yr gap, junior's early career).</li>
            </ul>
          </li>
          <li><strong>Big black-outlined dots</strong> are "heads of school" with 25+ descendants. There are 5,517 of them; the largest single lineage (Peter H. Rossi) spans 4,491 descendants.</li>
        </ul>
        <p>Click any dot to isolate that scholar's intellectual lineage — mentors in purple flowing up, mentees in green flowing down.</p>
      </section>

      <section class="cs-section">
        <h2>A note on missingness</h2>
        <ul>
          <li><strong>Anglophone journals only.</strong> Bourdieu doesn't have a chair here. Habermas doesn't either.</li>
          <li><strong>Co-authorship inference misses solo influence.</strong> Coleman, Granovetter, and Tilly trained whole generations through reading, not co-writing. Their footprint here looks smaller than their actual influence.</li>
          <li><strong>Recent students are sparse.</strong> Famous mentors have 40+ named PhD students on their CVs, but many haven't yet accumulated enough citations to land in the universe. They'll show up in 5–10 years.</li>
          <li><strong>Pre-1980 author disambiguation is noisier.</strong> OpenAlex's record-linking gets more uncertain for older publications, so some of the largest pre-1980 trees may absorb name-collisions ("John Smith, 1955" vs "John Smith, 1985"). Spot-checking the very largest trees is recommended before drawing strong conclusions about any one descendant chain.</li>
        </ul>
        <p>The viz makes this gap <em>visible</em>. Click any famous-but-childless author and you'll see the citation-vs-genealogy callout.</p>
      </section>

      <section class="cs-section">
        <h2>Method, briefly</h2>
        <dl class="cs-method">
          <dt>Authors + citations + journals</dt>
          <dd>OpenAlex Works API; curated 87-journal whitelist, top-cited works 1950–2025.</dd>
          <dt>Inferred mentor edges</dt>
          <dd>OpenAlex co-author pairs in same whitelist; Sinatra-style asymmetry rule (≥5yr first-pub gap, early-career joint paper). <a href="/sinatra-2016-memo.html">Methodology memo →</a></dd>
          <dt>Verified mentor edges</dt>
          <dd>Wikipedia infobox fields (<code>doctoral_students</code>, <code>doctoral_advisor</code>, <code>notable_students</code>) on top-500 authors.</dd>
          <dt>CV-verified edges (rolling)</dt>
          <dd>Direct CV scrapes for non-Wikipedia authors; regex on "DISSERTATION ADVISING" / "PhD Students" sections, role-tagged (chair / cochair / committee).</dd>
          <dt>Layout</dt>
          <dd>Width-weighted DFS tree rooted at earliest-mentor per component; Y = first publication year; tilt-fold for over-wide subtrees. Python + NetworkX + Plotly Scattergl.</dd>
        </dl>
        <p>The whole pipeline is resumable. Every time I sit at the coffee shop I add a few more chairs.</p>
      </section>

      <details class="cs-collapsible">
        <summary>Latest session — 2026-05-10</summary>
        <div class="cs-collapsible-body">
          <p><strong>16 CV-verified mentor → mentee edges</strong>, sourced from CVs, lab pages, and dissertation pages of an initial 17-scholar Latino-politics roster plus three calibration anchors (Mendelberg, Meier, Pérez).</p>
          <ul>
            <li><strong>Tali Mendelberg → Claire Willeck</strong> (chair, Princeton). Diss: <em>Active Civics: How Civic Education Shapes Political Engagement</em>. Won 2024 APSA E. E. Schattschneider Award. Committee: Mendelberg, Markus Prior, Andy Guess, Rocio Titiunik.</li>
            <li><strong>Efrén Pérez → 5 current PhDs in REPS Lab</strong> at UCLA: Ramona Alhambra, Gustavo A. Mártir Luna, Kasheena G. Rogbeer, Sydney Tran, Tricia Huynh.</li>
            <li><strong>Michael Jones-Correa → Angie Ocampo-Roland</strong> (chair, Penn 2021) — <em>inferred</em>, pending CV verification.</li>
          </ul>
          <p><strong>One correction worth flagging.</strong> First-pass surface search guessed Jason Casellas as Sam Chapa's dissertation chair at Houston. Her actual chair is <strong>Lydia Tiede</strong>. The verification pass caught it from her dissertation page — exactly the failure mode the audit step is designed to catch.</p>
          <p><strong>187 dissertation-cited authors</strong> extracted from the Worked Cited section of my dissertation. 12 of the 17 Phase-1 roster members appear in it; 25 cited-3×-or-more authors are Tier-2 promotion candidates (Barreto leads at 8, Segura 6, Masuoka and Pérez 5).</p>
        </div>
      </details>

      <details class="cs-collapsible">
        <summary>Expansion crawl — 2026-05-10</summary>
        <div class="cs-collapsible-body">
          <p>Sat down at the coffee shop and counted everyone in the room. Filter: <strong>US-affiliated authors with i10-index ≥ 2 publishing in any of 121 OpenAlex topics under "Political Science and International Relations" + "Public Administration."</strong></p>
          <p>Crawl pulled <strong>43,953 unique authors</strong>. Three filter tiers:</p>
          <ul class="cs-tier-list">
            <li><strong>Broad — 43,423.</strong> Max recall; includes cross-discipline scholars.</li>
            <li><strong>Subfield-proxy — 20,466.</strong> Top topic in PoliSci/IR/Soc+PoliSci/PubAdmin.</li>
            <li><strong>Concept-tight — 9,724.</strong> Top concept = Politics / Political science / PubAdmin / IR. Top 15 by h-index all real political scientists.</li>
          </ul>
          <p><strong>Top of the concept-tight list:</strong> Fukuyama, Keohane, Norris, John C. Campbell, Ken Meier, Bozeman, B. Guy Peters, Skocpol, Moynihan, Erikson, Iyengar, John W. Meyer, Tarrow, Apple.</p>
          <p><strong>Top institutions:</strong> Yale (87), Columbia (81), Wisconsin (76), UNC + UT Austin + American (73), Georgetown (71), NYU (70), Stanford + GW (69), Ohio State (67), Princeton + Cornell (66), Duke + Penn State (65), UCLA + Rutgers (62), Hopkins (61).</p>
          <p><strong>What this enabled.</strong> Originally ~7,000 dots filtered to a 87-journal flagship whitelist. Expansion added authors who publish outside that whitelist but still meet the impact bar.</p>
        </div>
      </details>

      <details class="cs-collapsible">
        <summary>Push to 1950 — 2026-05-12</summary>
        <div class="cs-collapsible-body">
          <p>Extended the corpus back another 30 years. A delta pull on the 87-journal flagship whitelist returned 85,472 pre-1980 works and 42,196 unique pre-1980 authors. After merging with the existing 1980+ universe and dropping junk records, the author dataset grew from 15,787 → <strong>40,012</strong>.</p>
          <p>Re-trimming the top-10,000 master tier brought in <strong>1,022 senior figures</strong> who didn't have enough post-1980 citations to qualify before — including Barney Glaser + Anselm Strauss (grounded theory), Harold Garfinkel (ethnomethodology), Peter L. Berger, Howard S. Becker, Alvin W. Gouldner, Thomas Luckmann, Rosabeth Moss Kanter, Otis Dudley Duncan, Elihu Katz, Fritz Heider. A focused coauthor crawl on those 1,022 newcomers added their lineage edges to the graph.</p>
          <p>Final shape: <strong>~39,500 dots</strong> plotted, <strong>5,517 heads of school</strong>, <strong>~54,400 visible mentor → mentee lines</strong>. Largest single lineage tree spans 4,491 descendants (Peter H. Rossi, sociology of evaluation research). The 1950s, 1960s, and 1970s decades now anchor the top of the canvas; Coleman, Parsons, Merton, Lipset, Dahl appear where they belong.</p>
          <p><strong>The delta approach saved hours.</strong> A full re-pull of the 1950–2025 range would have taken ~3–5 hours; pulling only the 1950–1979 slice and merging it into the existing dataset took ~15 minutes. The bobbish principle in action: don't fetch what you already have.</p>
        </div>
      </details>

      <details class="cs-collapsible">
        <summary>Flagged for soon, not now</summary>
        <div class="cs-collapsible-body">
          <p><strong>Non-US scholars.</strong> UK + Canada + Australia (~half the US count), Continental Europe (~similar magnitude), Latin America, East and South Asia, Africa. Going global multiplies the universe by roughly 2.5–3×, with uneven OpenAlex coverage outside the Anglophone core.</p>
          <p><strong>Books in the corpus.</strong> Currently the pipeline is journal-only. Adding monographs would surface scholars whose work lives mostly in books — Skocpol, Putnam, Theda Bell, Lipset — and would lift book-forward fields like political theory. Plausible 30K–60K additional title-level records to integrate.</p>
          <p><strong>Click-into-lineage UI.</strong> The current viz isolates one scholar's lineage on click. The next step is a side-panel "neighborhood" view that pulls grad students, coauthors, and book authors for the clicked scholar from a lazy-loaded per-author JSON. Architecture is planned; data scaffolding is in place.</p>
        </div>
      </details>

      <footer class="cs-footer">
        <p><em>Built with Claude. Re-framed as a coffee shop because the actual phrase "co-authorship-asymmetry-inferred mentorship network" makes most people stop reading at "co-".</em></p>
      </footer>

    </div>
  </aside>

  <button class="cs-toggle" id="csToggle" aria-controls="csSidebar" aria-expanded="true" title="Hide info panel">
    <span class="cs-toggle-icon" aria-hidden="true">‹</span>
    <span class="cs-toggle-text">Hide info</span>
  </button>

  <div class="cs-viz-wrap">
    <iframe class="cs-viz"
            src="/citation_timeline.html"
            title="Coffee-shop network: sociology & political science genealogy, 1950–2025"
            loading="lazy"></iframe>
  </div>
</div>

<style>
  :root {
    --cs-bg: #f7f3ea;
    --cs-card: #ffffff;
    --cs-ink: #14161a;
    --cs-muted: #6b665c;
    --cs-accent: #1e3a5f;
    --cs-soft: #efe9d6;
    --cs-border: #e3dccb;
    --cs-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(20, 22, 26, 0.04);
    --cs-sidebar-w: 320px;
    --cs-nav-h: 56px;
  }
  [data-bs-theme="dark"] {
    --cs-bg: #14161a;
    --cs-card: #1c1f25;
    --cs-ink: #e9e7df;
    --cs-muted: #9e9a8e;
    --cs-accent: #8eb4e3;
    --cs-soft: #2a2e38;
    --cs-border: #2f3340;
  }

  .cs-page {
    display: flex;
    height: calc(100vh - var(--cs-nav-h));
    overflow: hidden;
    background: var(--cs-bg);
    color: var(--cs-ink);
    font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif;
    position: relative;
  }

  .cs-sidebar {
    flex: 0 0 var(--cs-sidebar-w);
    width: var(--cs-sidebar-w);
    border-right: 1px solid var(--cs-border);
    background: var(--cs-card);
    overflow-y: auto;
    overflow-x: hidden;
    transition: flex-basis 0.28s ease, width 0.28s ease;
  }
  .cs-page.collapsed .cs-sidebar {
    flex-basis: 0;
    width: 0;
    border-right: none;
  }
  .cs-sidebar-inner {
    padding: 1.5rem 1.5rem 2rem;
    width: var(--cs-sidebar-w);
    box-sizing: border-box;
  }

  .cs-header h1 {
    margin: 0 0 0.25rem;
    font-size: 22px;
    font-weight: 500;
    letter-spacing: -0.01em;
    color: var(--cs-ink);
  }
  .cs-meta {
    margin: 0 0 0.5rem;
    font-size: 12px;
    color: var(--cs-muted);
    line-height: 1.4;
  }
  .cs-stats {
    margin: 0 0 1rem;
    font-size: 12.5px;
    color: var(--cs-muted);
  }
  .cs-stat-num {
    color: var(--cs-accent);
    font-weight: 500;
  }

  .cs-epigraph {
    margin: 0 0 1.5rem;
    padding: 0.75rem 1rem;
    border-left: 3px solid var(--cs-accent);
    background: var(--cs-soft);
    border-radius: 0 4px 4px 0;
    font-size: 13.5px;
    color: var(--cs-ink);
    line-height: 1.5;
  }

  .cs-section {
    margin: 0 0 1.5rem;
    padding-bottom: 1.5rem;
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--cs-border);
    font-size: 13.5px;
    line-height: 1.55;
    color: var(--cs-ink);
  }
  .cs-section:last-of-type { border-bottom: none; padding-bottom: 0; }
  .cs-section h2 {
    margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
    font-size: 14.5px;
    font-weight: 500;
    color: var(--cs-accent);
  }
  .cs-section p { margin: 0 0 0.75rem; }
  .cs-section p:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }
  .cs-section ul {
    margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
    padding-left: 1.1rem;
  }
  .cs-section li {
    margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
  }
  .cs-section li ul {
    margin-top: 0.4rem;
    padding-left: 1rem;
  }
  .cs-method {
    margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
  }
  .cs-method dt {
    font-weight: 500;
    color: var(--cs-accent);
    margin-top: 0.6rem;
    font-size: 12.5px;
  }
  .cs-method dt:first-child { margin-top: 0; }
  .cs-method dd {
    margin: 0.15rem 0 0;
    font-size: 12.5px;
    color: var(--cs-muted);
    line-height: 1.5;
  }
  .cs-method code {
    font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace;
    background: var(--cs-soft);
    padding: 1px 4px;
    border-radius: 3px;
    font-size: 11px;
  }

  .cs-collapsible {
    margin: 1.5rem 0 0;
    padding: 0.75rem 1rem;
    background: var(--cs-soft);
    border: 1px solid var(--cs-border);
    border-radius: 6px;
    font-size: 13px;
  }
  .cs-collapsible summary {
    cursor: pointer;
    font-weight: 500;
    color: var(--cs-accent);
    list-style: none;
    position: relative;
    padding-left: 1.2rem;
  }
  .cs-collapsible summary::-webkit-details-marker { display: none; }
  .cs-collapsible summary::before {
    content: "▸";
    position: absolute;
    left: 0;
    color: var(--cs-muted);
    transition: transform 0.15s;
  }
  .cs-collapsible[open] summary::before {
    transform: rotate(90deg);
  }
  .cs-collapsible-body {
    margin-top: 0.75rem;
    padding-top: 0.75rem;
    border-top: 1px solid var(--cs-border);
    line-height: 1.55;
    color: var(--cs-ink);
  }
  .cs-collapsible-body p { margin: 0 0 0.6rem; }
  .cs-collapsible-body p:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }
  .cs-collapsible-body ul {
    margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
    padding-left: 1.1rem;
  }
  .cs-collapsible-body li { margin-bottom: 0.4rem; }
  .cs-tier-list li strong { color: var(--cs-accent); }

  .cs-footer {
    margin-top: 1.5rem;
    padding-top: 1rem;
    border-top: 1px solid var(--cs-border);
    font-size: 11.5px;
    color: var(--cs-muted);
    line-height: 1.5;
  }
  .cs-footer p { margin: 0; }

  .cs-viz-wrap {
    flex: 1;
    min-width: 0;
    position: relative;
    background: var(--cs-bg);
  }
  .cs-viz {
    width: 100%;
    height: 100%;
    border: 0;
    display: block;
  }

  .cs-toggle {
    position: absolute;
    top: 14px;
    left: calc(var(--cs-sidebar-w) - 14px);
    z-index: 20;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 6px;
    padding: 6px 12px 6px 8px;
    background: var(--cs-card);
    border: 1px solid var(--cs-border);
    border-radius: 999px;
    color: var(--cs-accent);
    font-size: 12.5px;
    font-weight: 500;
    cursor: pointer;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(20, 22, 26, 0.06);
    transition: left 0.28s ease, background 0.15s, transform 0.15s;
    font-family: inherit;
  }
  .cs-toggle:hover {
    background: var(--cs-soft);
  }
  .cs-toggle:active {
    transform: scale(0.97);
  }
  .cs-toggle-icon {
    display: inline-block;
    width: 14px;
    text-align: center;
    transition: transform 0.28s ease;
    font-size: 14px;
    line-height: 1;
  }
  .cs-page.collapsed .cs-toggle {
    left: 14px;
  }
  .cs-page.collapsed .cs-toggle-icon {
    transform: rotate(180deg);
  }

  @media (max-width: 768px) {
    :root { --cs-sidebar-w: 300px; --cs-nav-h: 48px; }
    .cs-sidebar {
      position: absolute;
      top: 0; left: 0; bottom: 0;
      z-index: 15;
      box-shadow: 2px 0 16px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12);
    }
    .cs-page.collapsed .cs-sidebar {
      transform: translateX(-100%);
    }
  }
</style>

<script>
  (function() {
    var KEY = 'cs-sidebar-collapsed';
    var page = document.getElementById('csPage');
    var toggle = document.getElementById('csToggle');
    var sidebar = document.getElementById('csSidebar');
    if (!page || !toggle || !sidebar) return;

    function apply(collapsed) {
      page.classList.toggle('collapsed', collapsed);
      toggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', String(!collapsed));
      toggle.setAttribute('title', collapsed ? 'Show info panel' : 'Hide info panel');
      var textEl = toggle.querySelector('.cs-toggle-text');
      if (textEl) textEl.textContent = collapsed ? 'Info' : 'Hide info';
    }

    var saved = null;
    try { saved = localStorage.getItem(KEY); } catch (e) {}
    apply(saved === 'true');

    toggle.addEventListener('click', function() {
      var nextCollapsed = !page.classList.contains('collapsed');
      apply(nextCollapsed);
      try { localStorage.setItem(KEY, String(nextCollapsed)); } catch (e) {}
    });
  })();
</script>
```
